execution_summary: The Docker environment was built from python:3.10-slim with pandas, numpy, and scipy. The script replicate_analysis.py loaded the cleaned Qualtrics CSV, reconstructed the framing condition, filtered participants who passed the comprehension check, and ran a Welch t-test plus Cohen’s d. Execution succeeded with no deviations from preregistration.

code_executed.logs: Lottery n=82, Gift n=86. Means 17.28 vs 186.22. Welch t = -1.48, p = 0.143. Cohen’s d = -0.22. Sensitivity (WTP≤20): n=71 vs 76, means 12.71 vs 10.99, t = 1.53, p = 0.129, d = 0.25.
code_executed.environment: Python 3.10, pandas 1.5+, numpy 1.23+, scipy 1.9+ inside docker image replication-exec

results.hypothesis_tested: Participants in the lottery-ticket frame will have a lower mean WTP than those in the gift-certificate frame (after comprehension check).

findings_summary.outcome_name: Sensitivity: Mean WTP difference (gift – lottery) for WTP ≤ 20
